workspace_id comes from the authenticated user's session — not from the request body.
Returns run_id immediately — frontend polls for status.

Legacy fallback: scans task_succeeded event messages for "job_report_id=" / "fit_report_id=".

DiscoveryHardConstraints.title: DiscoveryHardConstraints
DiscoveryHardConstraints.description:
Explicit constraints the user provided.

All fields are optional. An absent field means the user did not specify
that constraint — the platform must NOT infer a default value for it.

FailedValidatorSummary.title: FailedValidatorSummary
FailedValidatorSummary.description: One validator's failure detail within a needs_review result_summary.

raw_user_request.title: Raw User Request
raw_user_request.description: User's natural language description of what they are looking for.

JobDiscoveryFrontendInput.title: JobDiscoveryFrontendInput
JobDiscoveryFrontendInput.description:
The structured frontend payload for a job_discovery run.

Placed into RunCreate.input_snapshot by the frontend.
The worker parses this before calling the IntentTranslator.

Profile, catalog context, and strategy context are NOT here —
they are injected by the worker/planner from platform state.

JobRead.title: JobRead
JobRead.description: A canonical job record returned by the API.

RunCreate.title: RunCreate
RunCreate.description: Request body for POST /api/app/runs — discriminated by run_type.

RunResultSummary.title: RunResultSummary
RunResultSummary.description:
Structured diagnostic payload written by the worker at run completion.

Populated for both terminal statuses:
  - validation_status="passed"  → run succeeded; candidate_count / job_ids present
  - validation_status="failed"  → run needs_review; phase / failed_validators present

All fields except validation_status are Optional because the exact set depends
on which execution phase produced the summary.
